λ

Wave Nature Explorer

Discover the quantum world where particles become waves

🎓 Learning Goals
  • • Understand de Broglie wavelength: λ = h / mv
  • • Connect math to physical meaning
  • • Explore wave-particle duality
Level 1: Guided Exploration
Step 1/4
e⁻
Electron
λ
⟶
Wave Nature
9.11 × 10⁻³¹ kg
Electron Proton Atom Molecule Baseball
1 × 10⁶ m/s
Slow Fast
λ = h / (m × v)
h = 6.626 × 10⁻³⁴ J·s (Planck's constant)
λ = 7.27 × 10⁻¹⁰ m
About the size of an atom - wave nature is significant!
💡
Try This!

Start by observing the electron. Notice how its wavelength compares to atomic sizes. Now slowly increase the mass using the slider. What happens to the wavelength?

Level 2: Challenge Mode
Score: 0/5
🔬

Challenge 1

An electron is moving at 2 × 10⁶ m/s. If we double its velocity, what happens to its de Broglie wavelength?

✓
Correct!
Since λ = h/(mv), doubling velocity means the wavelength is halved. The relationship is inversely proportional!
Remember: λ = h / (m × v)
Level 3: Sandbox Lab

🧪 Particle Designer

📊 Wave Visualization

e⁻
⟶
Mass: 9.11 × 10⁻³¹ kg
Velocity: 1.00 × 10⁶ m/s
Momentum: 9.11 × 10⁻²⁵ kg·m/s
λ (wavelength): 7.27 × 10⁻¹⁰ m

🔍 Analysis

Size Comparison
Your wavelength is about the size of an atom.
Wave Nature Significance
High
Observable Effects
✓ Diffraction through crystals
✓ Interference patterns
✓ Quantum tunneling possible
